threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still take the bus to visit the West End, instead of hopping on the overground for a quicker route to Shoreditch.
Caught a night bus from Peckham last week, and a guy was rapping Shakespeare to the beat of a passing car. Only in South London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an elderly man feeding the pigeons in Hermannplatz, while a street musician played an accordion right next to him. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you'll spot food trucks serving gourmet döner like it's haute cuisine. It's the only place where kebab can have a Michelin star vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they instinctively know which BART station to avoid on a Friday evening after hearing about another brake overheating incident.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Witnessed a lady in a pink feather boa arguing with a bus driver over the scent of her perfume—only in the TL, folks.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think it's all about Gaudí. But the real magic is in Gràcia's hidden plazas, where the locals sip vermut and share gossip, far from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map, staring at it like it's a treasure map, while the locals glide past without a second glance.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is reliable. Welcome to Dalston, where it’s always five minutes late, even at 3 AM.
Last night on the 343, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Adele while a cat curled up in a seat. Only in South London, eh?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sat at the Neukölln Arcaden when two sparrows landed right on my table, pecking at my leftover Döner. That's when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can literally find a vegan kebab shop next to a traditional döner place. The scent clash alone is worth the visit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their indifference to the BART delays. They know it’s not a matter of if, but when the chaos hits.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy catch a 47 while holding a burrito in one hand and a saxophone with the other. Only in the Tenderloin, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí and cafés, but the late-night bocadillos at local bars are where the real culture thrives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still scan the Metro map like it's an ancient treasure map, taking way too long to find their stop.
